122632.explanation: Ridderkerk residents should also be able to participate in decision-making on important issues between municipal elections. Party 18PLUS has used this instrument several times itself, such as with the petition Against a New Asylum Seeker Center and on one-way or two-way traffic in the center.

122633.explanation: Managing sports facilities and public green spaces is costly and time-consuming. Residents should not be burdened with these responsibilities.

122635.explanation: In Ridderkerk, this is already well arranged for hospitality owners. Since the 2022-2026 council period, all hospitality businesses in Ridderkerk are allowed to open on Sundays. Previously, hospitality on Sint Jorisplein was not permitted to do so.

122636.explanation: In Ridderkerk, an attractive loan is available for the purchase of, for example, solar panels, batteries, etc. This loan has a lower interest rate than through a bank or the national government.

122637.explanation: Outside existing industrial estates, it should no longer be possible to build a distribution center. The new large distribution center at the Ringdijk was permitted according to the zoning plan, but new housing would have been more appropriate there.

122638.explanation: However, Party 18PLUS has been receiving complaints since 2021 from residents and entrepreneurs about the behavior of some asylum boat inhabitants. The Ridderkerk municipality has taken responsibility but also experiences nuisance. Party 18PLUS opposes the arrival of a new asylum seeker center.

122640.explanation: It is important that residents familiarize themselves with the Heating Guide to burn responsibly, so that nuisance to neighbors is limited.

122643.explanation: Party 18PLUS wants bulky household waste to be collected free of charge once a month again in the neighborhoods. This way residents can take these moments into account, making the streets less messy. The current system fails and causes a very untidy public space.

122644.explanation: Many events are organized in Ridderkerk that attract many Ridderkerk residents. The annual RadioNL festival is a great success and would be less successful if noise standards were stricter. Neighbors also enjoy partying along.

122646.explanation: In Ridderkerk, this is already made possible through a budget on the Ridderkerk pass.

122649.explanation: In Ridderkerk, the ToBe Foundation is active, offering music lessons. Also, the Facet Ridderkerk Foundation offers activities with music lessons.

122652.explanation: Since 2024, the Ridderkerk municipality again has its own civil service organization with its own enforcement officers (boa's). Before 2024, Ridderkerk had two to three boa's available. Now there are eight. With ten boa's, enforcement can also take place at night and on Sundays.

122653.explanation: Camera surveillance helps prevent and reduce crime and nuisance.

122660.explanation: Subsidy is not necessary. The former Rabobank building has now been demolished and beautiful apartments will be built there. A developer can do this without municipal subsidy. Also, new apartments will be built above the Hema in the former library building, also without municipal subsidy.

122661.explanation: There will be flexible homes for various target groups such as urgent Ridderkerk seekers including students. The target group for temporary housing is larger, for example people in divorce. Focusing only on students is, according to Party 18PLUS, undesirable.

122662.explanation: This is one of the 10 key points of Party 18PLUS: initially helping these people to find a job and, in the meantime, having them provide a counter-performance.

122663.explanation: Residents receiving social assistance benefits already receive money from the community to pay for primary matters including municipal taxes. In addition to social assistance benefits, they receive a Ridderkerk pass with an annual credit of hundreds of euros.

122666.explanation: Party 18PLUS sees opportunities to charge cars using the cheaper electricity at home with special cable ducts under the sidewalk.

122667.explanation: On main roads such as Populierenlaan, Vlietlaan, and Koninginneweg, the speed limit may remain 50 km/h. The vast majority of streets in Ridderkerk are already 30 km/h zones. Enforcement on these is more urgent.

122669.explanation: The center of Ridderkerk cannot be compared with the center of Rotterdam. In Ridderkerk, such a patronizing rule would yield little environmental benefit.

122670.explanation: That freedom must exist. If this costs extra money, then the costs should be borne by the resident who chooses it themselves.

122671.explanation: Youth care in Ridderkerk costs the Ridderkerk community annually between 10 and 20 million euros. This also includes caregivers who make huge profits from youth care.

122673.explanation: Ridderkerk is already (well) below the regional and national average.

122676.explanation: Since its foundation, Party 18PLUS has held the position that shop owners themselves may decide whether their store in Ridderkerk is open or closed on Sundays.

122677.explanation: Status holders should no longer receive urgency status for a social rental home in Ridderkerk. Ridderkerk residents sometimes wait ten years or longer for a social rental home. That is simply not fair and therefore status holders should no longer receive urgency.

122680.explanation: Ridderkerk children with parents on low incomes can already obtain swimming diploma A for free with the Ridderkerk pass. Another great position of Party 18PLUS that has been realized.

122681.explanation: It is up to an entrepreneur to decide whether there is a market for this in Ridderkerk. Previous coffeeshops in Ridderkerk have eventually closed. In Rotterdam, there are many coffeeshops where Ridderkerk residents can also go.

122683.explanation: Party 18PLUS finds a maximum building height of six floors appropriate to the village character of Ridderkerk.

122684.explanation: This could be a solution for Ridderkerk students. Friends housing could also provide a solution. Individual rooms should not be rented to migrant workers.

122689.explanation: There is a need in Ridderkerk for affordable owner-occupied homes and middle-priced rental homes. Ridderkerk already has a high percentage of social rental homes compared to surrounding municipalities and much higher than the national average.

122632.explanation: Elections give the municipal council the mandate to make decisions. The SGP supports involving residents through citizen participation. This ensures careful and transparent decision-making.

122633.explanation: Public facilities must be managed integrally.

122635.explanation: No, the SGP believes it should remain as it currently is.

122636.explanation: The municipality offers a sustainability loan for this purpose.

122637.explanation: The SGP wants to maintain the current zoning plan division of the municipality.

122638.explanation: The SGP believes we must help people in need, where the shelter is balanced with the fair interests of the local population and the responsibility is fairly shared among all municipalities.

122640.explanation: The SGP supports sober, realistic plans that contribute to a clean and livable environment in the long term.

122643.explanation: The SGP advocates adhering to existing agreements.

122644.explanation: Noise nuisance at outdoor events must be kept within limits.

122646.explanation: Low-income households are supported through the Ridderkerk Pass. The municipality actively supports sports clubs in providing sufficient, good, and sustainable sports opportunities. The SGP expects clubs to contribute socially so that, for example, low-income residents can also participate in activities.

122649.explanation: The SGP believes every child should have the opportunity to take music lessons. Schools receive support for nature and cultural education. The municipality gives them space for their own interpretation, fitting the school's identity.

122652.explanation: The current number of enforcement officers (boa's) is adequate, but their deployment can be more focused on safety where needed. For example, by increasing the visibility of neighborhood enforcement officers and strengthening cooperation with neighborhood police officers and neighborhood watch groups.

122653.explanation: Prevention is better than cure. Therefore, the SGP considers prevention crucial. Crime decreases when the chance of being caught increases. Preventive measures, such as neighborhood watch and camera surveillance (such as ANPR cameras on exit roads), help prevent problems.

122660.explanation: The municipality, together with housing corporations and project developers, commits to an appropriate housing supply. An important principle for renovation and new construction is to maximize the flow in the housing market. The municipality encourages the use of national and provincial subsidies.

122661.explanation: Given the current housing shortage, temporary housing is a good solution. But only for students and only when the homes are well integrated into the environment.

122662.explanation: Residents receiving benefits should in principle deliver a counter-performance, for example through volunteer work.

122663.explanation: The safety net is not a hammock, according to the SGP. Social security is intended as a temporary safety net for those who need it, not as a permanent arrangement for those who can work but choose not to. A generous minimum policy also includes exemption from local taxes.

122666.explanation: The SGP supports (more) (fast) charging stations in public spaces to encourage electric transport.

122667.explanation: The SGP attaches great importance to traffic safety. It is very important that everyone can participate safely in traffic. The roads to and around schools receive special attention.

122669.explanation: The SGP believes this measure does not fit Ridderkerk.

122670.explanation: The SGP wants residents to keep freedom of choice so they can receive care that fits their beliefs.

122671.explanation: Families are under great pressure. This demands critical use of municipal budgets, focusing on prevention and appropriate care. Good quality care contributes to reducing costs.

122673.explanation: Local taxes are not a cash cow. Only inflation correction is applied to property tax (ozb).

122676.explanation: The SGP believes that in a healthy local economy and a functioning social labor market, rest is also necessary from time to time. For our welfare and health, rest is indispensable. God in His wisdom has given us the blessing of a collective rest moment: Sunday.

122677.explanation: The shelter of status holders must be balanced with the fair interests of the local population.

122680.explanation: The SGP approves of the municipality promoting school swimming by keeping costs low. The municipality subsidizes the swimming pool.

122681.explanation: The SGP supports a strict drug policy and opposes the establishment of coffeeshops in Ridderkerk.

122683.explanation: The SGP believes that high-rise buildings are a way to add housing despite limited space. However, high-rise is not suitable in every part of Ridderkerk. For example, in the SGP's view, only low-rise should be allowed in Rijsoord and Oostendam. The 2025 building height vision provides a good framework for this.

122684.explanation: The SGP opposes renting out individual rooms, as this can undermine livability and peace in residential neighborhoods.

122689.explanation: The SGP supports an appropriate housing supply. A key principle for new construction is to maximize flow in the housing market. Another principle is the so-called 'More Balanced Scenario' (a maximum of 30 percent social housing). The current share is around 40 percent.

122632.explanation: Residents must certainly be able to express their opinions. A referendum only works if it is acted upon. So, not just as advice, but binding and enforceable.

122633.explanation: Small planting beds in one's own street can-provided clear agreements are made-be managed by residents so that the greenery remains well cared for and tidy. The management of a sports hall is better kept under municipal responsibility.

122635.explanation: Ridderkerk already has generous opening hours. You also need to consider the surroundings. It is not advisable to let hospitality venues determine their own opening hours.

122636.explanation: Generating sustainable energy together deserves support. However, financial support must be appropriate and remain within reasonable limits.

122637.explanation: That depends on the content of the proposal, construction plans, the impact on traffic, and the effects of this new building on the surroundings and residents. The same mistakes made with the arrival of Panattoni must not be repeated.

122638.explanation: Ridderkerk currently shelters about 90 refugees. Leefbaar Ridderkerk opposes further increases in numbers and also opposes building a new shelter location at the dredge depot.

122640.explanation: Wood burning should be allowed to a limited extent. Clear ongoing explanations about the burning guide must be provided so everyone knows when it is or isn't wise to burn.

122643.explanation: Collecting bulky waste free of charge twice a year or placing neighborhood containers can help reduce litter. This could really make a difference.

122644.explanation: At festivals, the municipality should be able to set noise limits. This prevents hearing damage and ensures consideration for local residents.

122646.explanation: Low-income residents can already participate in sports through the Ridderkerk Pass, and according to Leefbaar Ridderkerk, this should remain the case.

122649.explanation: Music is important in the development of children. If parents cannot afford it, there should be an option for a subsidy.

122652.explanation: Our enforcement officers do a lot of good work, even though not everyone always sees that. It is important to communicate better about all that they do. Together we ensure a safe neighborhood.

122653.explanation: ANPR cameras at the entrances of Ridderkerk. People who enter our village with bad intentions will be detected more quickly. Other camera surveillance only if strictly necessary.

122660.explanation: If a project developer wants to convert vacant offices into homes, a subsidy is not necessary. Full cooperation regarding permits, and so on, is required.

122661.explanation: No container homes, but flexible housing should be placed spread across the neighborhoods. They must fit into the surroundings and the neighborhood.

122662.explanation: To stay active and increase opportunities to return to work, it is wise to do socially meaningful work.

122663.explanation: People who are already financially struggling should not fall into debt or deeper into debt due to stopping the exemption from municipal taxes for residents with a low income.

122666.explanation: Only if there are more electric cars in the municipality should more parking spaces be designated as charging points. These must be genuinely valuable. Do not designate charging spots unnecessarily.

122667.explanation: 30 km/h where it is really needed, 50 km/h where necessary and it can be done safely. Preferably with separated bike lanes. Safety always comes first.

122669.explanation: An environmental zone for Ridderkerk is not necessary.

122670.explanation: Everyone should be able to choose a caregiver that suits them. Good appropriate care also means that someone feels comfortable and understood.

122673.explanation: Ridderkerk already has a low property tax (OZB). We will not increase it unless it is really necessary.

122676.explanation: Shopkeepers should be able to decide for themselves whether to open or not.

122677.explanation: No priority for status holders on social rental housing. We consciously choose not to give priority to groups, with at least 50 percent (legal requirement) reserved for our own residents.

122680.explanation: Only children from very low-income families should continue to receive support for swimming lessons.

122681.explanation: No coffee shop in our village!

122683.explanation: Leefbaar is not in favor of high-rise buildings. High-rise only if it fits within the existing residential area. The village character of Ridderkerk must be preserved.

122684.explanation: No permission for renting out individual rooms as far as Leefbaar Ridderkerk is concerned. Houses must remain available for families and other home seekers who need a complete home.

122689.explanation: Ridderkerk already has more than the required number of social rental homes. So no, unless they are only allowed to be built for Ridderkerk residents!

122632.explanation: In principle, not fundamentally against a corrective referendum (a referendum that can overturn laws), but only acceptable if it is well regulated with clear conditions and does not undermine the council’s governing and supervisory role.

122633.explanation: Managing a public garden is truly different from managing a sports hall. Both are possible only under strict conditions and agreements.

122635.explanation: Opening hours may depend on the environment where cafés and restaurants are located. This currently works well in Ridderkerk.

122636.explanation: Residents must receive support in any case. This can also be achieved by the municipality thinking along and facilitating. Financial support if necessary, for example through a sustainability loan.

122637.explanation: Opposing new initiatives upfront is not the right approach. New initiatives must be assessed and weighed on feasibility, desirability, and sufficient space.

122638.explanation: We follow the Distribution Act and take our responsibility as a municipality accordingly.

122640.explanation: The ChristianUnion is critical of wood burning when it causes nuisance to health and air quality, and wants citizens to be well informed about harmful effects and how to burn responsibly.

122643.explanation: We find the current tariff scheme very reasonable. The tariff aligns with rates in other municipalities in the region and free disposal is possible at the waste facility.

122644.explanation: There are existing guidelines and signals from previous events and festivals give no reason to adjust these guidelines.

122646.explanation: The CU does not see sport as a luxury, but as an important social provision. Therefore, we advocate for municipal schemes that reimburse or subsidize sports memberships. In Ridderkerk, this is already arranged through the Ridderkerk pass.

122649.explanation: Music lessons should primarily be given at primary school and already are. No municipal subsidy is needed for this. Learning to play an instrument is already subsidized, among others via the Ridderkerk pass.

122652.explanation: In recent times, extra public order officers have been added in Ridderkerk. It is important that in the coming council term efforts are made for more visible presence in public spaces.

122653.explanation: The CU regards camera surveillance as a serious measure that may only be used: For serious and structural safety problems (e.g. violence, nuisance, crime), when lighter measures demonstrably do not work (such as extra supervision, neighborhood prevention, or social approach).

122660.explanation: Vacant offices offer opportunities for new homes. Conversion does not necessarily require a subsidy from the municipality. The municipality should mainly facilitate and assist initiatives.

122661.explanation: The municipality should primarily build a varied range of homes. Housing for young people and/or students is part of this.

122662.explanation: People on social assistance should not be left on the sidelines. Participation in volunteer work, neighborhood activities, or social engagement can help with meaning, integration, rhythm, and social contacts. However, social work must never be mandatory as a condition for benefits.

122663.explanation: Municipal taxes are not a punishment and should not lead to debt problems. Exemption is part of poverty policy, not a favor. We see exemption as debt prevention, protection of vulnerable households, and dignified governance.

122666.explanation: The ChristianUnion supports expanding charging infrastructure. But with attention to phased growth (growing with the number of electric cars), good monitoring of parking pressure, and smart placement (not 'the same standard everywhere').

122667.explanation: Positive as a standard from the perspective of sustainability, safety, and health. On main roads, 50 km/h can remain as the speed to enable traffic flow.

122669.explanation: We consider care for the environment and climate important; we endorse that every municipality must commit to CO₂ reduction, cleaner air, and sustainable mobility. We believe that introducing an environmental zone as a measure fits better in a strongly urbanized living environment.

122670.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes that care must be people-centered. People should be able to choose a healthcare provider that matches their faith, culture, or beliefs. This is essential for dignified, personal care and trust.

122671.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes that municipalities should not primarily focus on price in youth care. Quality, continuity, and the child's best interest must be leading. Too strong a focus on costs harms good care.

122673.explanation: The property tax (ozb) in Ridderkerk is low compared to the region and increases annually only slightly in line with the growth in service level. The property tax should not rise without reason. But any increase should not be deferred too far into the future. A balance with the level of services is important.

122676.explanation: The ChristenUnie attaches great importance to Sunday rest and sees Sunday as a day of rest, meeting, and reflection.

122677.explanation: The ChristenUnie supports priority for status holders out of humanity and the importance of integration, but wants to combine this with extra (temporary) housing construction and fair solutions for all housing seekers.

122680.explanation: The current participation scheme allows for a swimming lesson credit for low-income individuals. Consideration could possibly be given to extending this to free swimming lessons for low-income individuals up to 130 percent of the social assistance standard.

122681.explanation: Municipalities formally have policy space to allow establishment. ChristenUnie believes that space should be used to keep out coffeeshops. We want to protect our youth and their health and counteract nuisance and undermining.

122683.explanation: The ChristenUnie sees advantages in taller residential buildings when it contributes to affordable housing and a varied supply, fits urban locations with good infrastructure (public transport, amenities). Integration and improvement of spatial quality are important considerations.

122684.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes that Ridderkerk can allow rooming houses if it is part of a targeted and regulated policy, fits the composition and resilience of the neighborhood, and there are good quality standards and enforcement. We oppose uncontrolled subdivision in existing neighborhoods.

122689.explanation: Housing is not a luxury but must remain accessible. Investments in social rental housing must be part of a broader housing strategy, also paying attention to middle rental, flow-through, speeding up building procedures, and sufficient construction pace.

122632.explanation: Provided this is an advisory referendum. Residents already choose their representatives themselves. These representatives must stay in touch with their constituency and know what is going on. An advisory referendum is a good form of participation on important issues.

122633.explanation: In itself, this is an interesting option. The challenge is to ensure continuity and the right people, with the right qualifications, to take over management. If everything is guaranteed, it should be possible. But not all management lends itself to this. Residents are often volunteers; it should not be done casually.

122635.explanation: This is undesirable. This type of activity is often located near residents' homes, which can cause nuisance if they decide to operate at unusual hours.

122636.explanation: The national government often already provides subsidies for this. Additionally, there is currently a problem that too much energy (electricity) is being generated and the power grid cannot handle it.

122637.explanation: Why should a municipality do this? A municipality should pay attention to the infrastructure such a center needs and the traffic handling that can be disadvantageous and cause nuisance. So do not just allow any center anywhere in the municipality.

122638.explanation: The current shelter is organized without much nuisance. We do oppose expansion and new construction to accommodate even more asylum seekers.

122640.explanation: Residents must have their own choice in this. The municipality is responsible for good information on how and when to use this correctly to avoid nuisance as much as possible.

122643.explanation: This could possibly be limited to two to four times per year per address in the municipality.

122644.explanation: This is to prevent nuisance to neighbors but also to prevent hearing damage.

122646.explanation: This is indirectly already done in Ridderkerk. People with benefits can use a Ridderkerk pass. With this pass, they can use the offerings and/or facilities of various sports clubs.

122649.explanation: If parents have sufficient financial means to finance this themselves, then it is no longer a task of the municipality.

122652.explanation: We currently have nine enforcement officers (boa's), but due to increasing crime and violations of normal rules, enforcement with this number is very difficult. So we believe more enforcement officers should be appointed.

122653.explanation: There are several locations in Ridderkerk where residents experience nuisance. Unfortunately, with current regulations, a mobile camera unit cannot simply be deployed. We would like to see this changed.

122660.explanation: There is a need for more housing. Vacant offices can reduce this need. Ridderkerk has already seen such a project succeed before. So why not elsewhere in the municipality? However, if project developers make a lot of money from it, subsidies are not necessarily needed.

122661.explanation: The municipality must ensure there are enough homes for starters, young people, families, and the elderly. These may also be temporary homes.

122662.explanation: Receiving benefits is unpleasant enough. But sitting at home without purpose or development opportunities is good for no one. People receive money from the state; they should give something back. Provided they are mentally/physically able; so according to the person's capacity.

122663.explanation: We must help people with low incomes to stay out of debt. One option is to forgive these taxes. However, look at how someone handles money. If money is spent on the wrong things, reduce the forgiveness. Otherwise, it is unfair to others.

122666.explanation: At this moment, this is sufficient. And if not, residents can apply for such a place.

122667.explanation: Not for main roads. On those, one must still be able to drive 50 km/h. Other roads can be reduced to 30 km/h.

122669.explanation: For now, this is not necessary, but if Ridderkerk residents experience much nuisance from polluting vehicles in the center, then discussion/consideration can still take place.

122670.explanation: Not always. There may be factors that limit the choice. Finances are one of them. One can strive for this principle and ensure it is realized as much as possible.

122671.explanation: Quality is more important than price. Through proper, timely assistance, the help process can be shortened and costs kept lower than if you focus solely on price.

122673.explanation: But the rate should not increase more than inflation.

122676.explanation: EvR has done research. The vast majority of retail wants Sunday to be closed. The large (supermarket) chains want to be open. Young people want a shopping Sunday; most (somewhat) older people do not. A national law is already coming for this. We think optionally once per month as a compromise is an option.

122677.explanation: They should not be given priority over our own residents who have often been registered for social rental housing for years. Many refugees come to the Netherlands via other safe countries. They had chances to settle elsewhere, in countries with fewer people and more space.

122680.explanation: The Netherlands is a watery country and many children can no longer get swimming lessons due to costs. This is a bad development. We must ensure together that all children at least obtain a B diploma.

122681.explanation: Because of nuisance and making 'soft drugs' use too easy, we are against it. We must try to reverse drug use rather than encourage it.

122683.explanation: But only where it fits in the environment and provided it does not become colossal buildings, but for example is built in a stepped (receding) manner, so it is not massive buildings. And preferably provided with evergreen plants on the outer walls. This is good against heat stress and for the environment.

122684.explanation: Where people have space and others can use that space, we can solve a housing shortage problem this way. This was also done immediately after World War II and is already allowed in other municipalities. However, it must not lead to 'slumlords'.

122689.explanation: There are enough of them. They just need to benefit Ridderkerk residents more. Currently only 42 percent of available homes are rented to local residents. That percentage must increase. We support a local rental mediation and management system.

122632.explanation: The CDA is against referendums. However, there should remain possibilities for residents to participate in decision-making, for example through participation.

122633.explanation: When such initiatives arise and are feasible, we should definitely encourage them.

122635.explanation: The opening hours for hospitality venues and cafés in Ridderkerk are generous. Not all entrepreneurs make use of these extended hours. The opening and closing times also serve to prevent nuisance for nearby residents. Within the set times, entrepreneurs can decide for themselves when they want to be open.

122636.explanation: As CDA, we are certainly enthusiastic about initiatives from society, and the costs should primarily be covered by the initiators themselves. If the initiative can succeed with a subsidy from the municipality, we view this positively.

122637.explanation: The arrival of Panattoni (Slikkerveer) was undesirable. We are committed to adjusting zoning plans (Environmental Plan) so that new distribution centers are not possible. CDA is critical of the establishment of new distribution centers in Ridderkerk.

122638.explanation: As a municipality, we have a legal task from the national government to accommodate asylum seekers. By choosing to cooperate, Ridderkerk ensures that the national government does not decide where asylum seekers are housed in our municipality, but that we retain influence ourselves.

122640.explanation: Through the stookwijzer (heating guide), residents of Ridderkerk are already informed about when it is or isn’t advisable to heat.

122643.explanation: Everyone is responsible for disposing of their own bulky waste. If you are unable to do so, the municipality collects it for 20 euros. For residents with a low income, bulky waste can be collected at home free of charge.

122644.explanation: According to the CDA, the current noise standards are adequate.

122646.explanation: The CDA stands for a healthy municipality and sports are essential to that. In addition, being a member of a (sports) club broadens social networks, and we encourage that.

122649.explanation: For children whose parents have no or insufficient funds for music lessons, we want to make this possible. Music lessons should be accessible to all children.

122652.explanation: The number of enforcement officers (boa's) has already increased recently. To adequately enforce traffic safety and prevent nuisances such as from fatbikes, additional officers are needed.

122653.explanation: The CDA supports additional camera surveillance, among others at (neighborhood) shopping centers and central bicycle parking facilities.

122660.explanation: The housing market is very tight, and converting vacant office buildings helps make more homes available to the residents of Ridderkerk.

122661.explanation: This is not the largest target group for which building should take place in Ridderkerk. But when temporary housing is built, these are of course also available for students from Ridderkerk.

122662.explanation: If possible for people on social assistance benefits, it is good to ask them to perform socially meaningful (volunteer) work in return.

122663.explanation: There is already a pilot project in Ridderkerk to purchase the debts of young people that is very effective and delivers good results.

122666.explanation: In large parts of Ridderkerk, it is often a challenge to park close to your home. Therefore, we want to place additional parking spaces for electric cars only when there is demand.

122667.explanation: In residential neighborhoods, we opt for a 30 km/h speed limit. Main and feeder roads remain 50 km/h to ensure that traffic flows well and safely there.

122669.explanation: This will also impact, for example, contractors' vans, which we do not consider desirable. According to the CDA, the effect in Ridderkerk is too small to introduce an environmental zone.

122670.explanation: It is important to choose a healthcare provider that fits your beliefs, and we want to maintain this. Because of scarcity, this is not always possible, and in those cases, receiving good care is the most important.

122671.explanation: Of course, costs must be taken into account, but the quality of care is at least as important.

122673.explanation: The property tax (ozb) may only increase as a result of an inflation adjustment.

122676.explanation: The CDA considers maintaining Sunday rest essential. One day a week of rest to spend with family, faith, sports, and volunteer work is important to us. Moreover, opening shops on Sunday negatively impacts smaller entrepreneurs.

122677.explanation: In our opinion, this priority rule is unfair to other housing seekers. Ridderkerk must fulfill the national government's assignment through direct mediation.

122680.explanation: Having a swimming diploma is essential in the Netherlands. Where there is a need for school swimming, the municipality must also allocate funds for this.

122681.explanation: The CDA was, is, and remains opposed to a coffeeshop in Ridderkerk. Drug use has, besides a negative impact on health, also a negative social impact. In our opinion, a coffeeshop only causes nuisance.

122683.explanation: The CDA believes that high-rise buildings are necessary to realize the needed additional housing. On the other hand, the village character of Ridderkerk must be preserved. Except for a few exceptions, we do not want buildings taller than six stories. In Rijsoord and Oostendam, we opt for a maximum of three stories.

122684.explanation: We are positive about friends’ housing and renting a room from a landlady/host. At the same time, we hear that room rentals sometimes cause nuisance. Therefore, we find it important to establish clear rules for room occupancy to ensure it remains pleasant and livable for everyone.

122689.explanation: In our opinion, 2000 additional homes must be built and/or be planned in the coming years. This also requires investment in social rental housing.

122632.explanation: VVD Ridderkerk is against referendums. Important decisions are often complex and require careful consideration. Elected representatives have the trust of residents to take that responsibility, with good participation and genuine input.

122633.explanation: The municipality is responsible for managing sports halls and parks. This ensures quality is maintained, with room for residents to think along and contribute.

122635.explanation: VVD Ridderkerk stands for the freedom of entrepreneurs and residents. Cafés and restaurants should be able to determine their own opening hours, also on Sundays. This also applies to shops. No one is forced, everyone has freedom of choice.

122636.explanation: VVD Ridderkerk believes financial support for sustainable energy is a national task. The municipality helps by keeping regulations simple, facilitating initiatives, and providing support during startup.

122637.explanation: Ridderkerk is full and space is scarce. The space that remains we want to use for housing, where there is great need. New distribution centers mainly cause extra heavy traffic.

122638.explanation: Asylum accommodation requires shared responsibility. Simply saying we don’t want it is unrealistic and unfair to residents. VVD Ridderkerk chooses clear agreements and a fair distribution to all municipalities, with safety as a precondition.

122640.explanation: Open fireplaces and wood stoves may remain. Fewer rules and more personal responsibility, with education and intervention in case of major nuisance.

122643.explanation: Nothing is free. A contribution may be requested for the collection of bulky waste.

122644.explanation: A party is part of it, especially for young people. VVD Ridderkerk believes there should be space for festivals and parties. They may sometimes be audible, with attention to the environment, without extra rules.

122646.explanation: Sports help people stay healthy and participate. Therefore, VVD Ridderkerk finds it important that children of people on social assistance benefits can play sports, supported through municipal schemes.

122649.explanation: Not everything needs to be subsidized. Parents arrange music lessons themselves. This way, we keep municipal expenses under control.

122652.explanation: Safety is non-negotiable. VVD Ridderkerk wants more enforcement officers on the streets, visible and approachable, so residents feel safe in their neighborhoods.

122653.explanation: Safety comes first. In places where there are risks and disturbances, camera surveillance is necessary to prevent crime and enhance safety.

122660.explanation: No subsidies, but speed. With a housing construction task force, VVD Ridderkerk wants to provide clarity and fast procedures so that vacant offices can be quickly converted into homes.

122661.explanation: VVD Ridderkerk supports temporary student housing, such as container homes, at suitable locations and for a limited period.

122662.explanation: Free money does not exist. Those who receive social assistance benefits should also give something back to society. VVD Ridderkerk believes it is logical for people, where possible, to perform socially meaningful work.

122663.explanation: VVD Ridderkerk wants to maintain debt forgiveness for residents with low incomes. For the municipality, this is a limited cost, while for people it makes a big difference to keep their finances afloat.

122666.explanation: Where demand grows, extra charging points are logical. Without coercion or green nannying. People choose their own car; the municipality ensures sufficient parking spaces.

122667.explanation: VVD Ridderkerk chooses tailored solutions. 30 km/h where necessary for safety, 50 km/h on main roads where traffic is designed for flow and visibility.

122669.explanation: VVD Ridderkerk opposes an environmental zone in the center. Such a measure limits accessibility for residents and entrepreneurs and mainly affects people who depend on their car.

122670.explanation: People can use the personal budget (PGB) to choose a care provider that fits their religion, culture, or beliefs.

122671.explanation: VVD Ridderkerk believes that youth care decisions should not be based solely on price. Quality and effectiveness should always weigh heavily, so children receive the care they need.

122673.explanation: VVD Ridderkerk advocates for lower housing costs. Taxes should go down instead of continuously increasing.

122676.explanation: Shops should simply be allowed to open on Sundays. Entrepreneurs and residents decide for themselves whether to make use of this. VVD Ridderkerk chooses freedom: your Sunday, your choice.

122677.explanation: VVD Ridderkerk believes everyone should be treated equally. Those who want a social rental home should simply wait their turn in the queue. No priority, just fair waiting for everyone.

122680.explanation: Swimming is an important basic skill. School swimming increases children's safety and helps prevent drowning. VVD Ridderkerk finds it logical that the municipality supports this.

122681.explanation: There is no plan for a coffee shop in Ridderkerk. If it ever becomes relevant, VVD Ridderkerk believes it should be allowed but only under strict rules and with attention to safety and livability.

122683.explanation: The housing shortage calls for smart solutions. VVD Ridderkerk is open to taller residential buildings, provided they are well integrated into the environment.

122684.explanation: The housing shortage is significant. Renting out individual rooms can be a good solution for people who don't need a full home, such as students or starters. VVD Ridderkerk is positive about this, provided it is done properly and without nuisance.

122689.explanation: Ridderkerk already has a large share of social rental housing. VVD Ridderkerk does not want to invest extra money in social housing for home seekers from other municipalities. We build what Ridderkerk needs: mid-rent, owner-occupied homes, and suitable housing for the elderly.

122632.explanation: The number of referendums should be limited and should concern municipality-wide topics.

122633.explanation: Partly agree. Maintenance of a park could be fine if it is facilitated by the municipality. Maintenance of a sports hall should not be left to residents. With real estate, this can become too complex or large.

122635.explanation: Partially agree. There should be a clear uniform closing time at night.

122636.explanation: These are wonderful initiatives that should be encouraged, facilitated, and possibly financially supported.

122637.explanation: We already have/have gotten too many of these. They are all ugly warehouses and cause so much traffic that the highways can no longer handle it, making Ridderkerk inaccessible to residents.

122638.explanation: Closure is not necessary, but large-scale expansion as currently planned is not needed either. No 250 people at one location. We currently have shelter for about 90 people; another shelter elsewhere for another 90 people should be possible, but no more.

122640.explanation: There should also be no enforcement on burning; we should keep that freedom. But we should fully promote burning according to the Burning Guide.

122643.explanation: This currently happens for a small fee, and bulky pruning waste is collected for free. Make bulky waste collection free periodically as well.

122644.explanation: The current arrangements are good, according to national standards.

122646.explanation: We have already arranged some things for this. Awareness of these opportunities needs to be increased.

122649.explanation: Every primary school child should at least have the opportunity to experience making music. This does not have to be directly subsidized by the municipality.

122652.explanation: We currently have an enforcement officer (BOA) for every neighborhood. Look at their tasks, deploy them effectively in and for the neighborhoods, and let them cooperate with neighborhood watch.

122653.explanation: Camera surveillance on the highways around Ridderkerk is certainly desirable, and we have co-submitted a motion for ANPR cameras.

122660.explanation: Focusing on this is sustainable. Sustainable in materials-also consider foundation-as well as sustainable in space usage.

122661.explanation: We are not a student 'city.' We can focus on housing for young people, for example, by splitting larger homes or making living in backyards easier.

122662.explanation: Forcing is not possible. People are in social assistance for various reasons. But encouraging them to do so is fine.

122663.explanation: A good rule that should remain.

122666.explanation: This could be possible, but these must remain free, public parking spaces and not parking spots exclusively for electric cars.

122667.explanation: Maximum speed may be reduced to 30 km/h in residential areas, but the access roads (the streets leading out of neighborhoods) should remain at 50 km/h to maintain traffic flow, allow buses to run on time, and enable emergency services to pass through.

122669.explanation: This restricts our self-employed persons and businesses. We must not want this.

122670.explanation: When it comes to household help, we find this appropriate. For other forms of care, you should leave it free.

122671.explanation: Focus especially on experience, quality, and size of the organization.

122676.explanation: We are against Sunday shopping because we believe that a day of 'rest' during the week is necessary for many. We think this will severely harm small independent businesses, which actually add character to our shopping areas.

122677.explanation: We gladly offer our residents and status holders the same chances of obtaining housing.

122680.explanation: This also applies to the schools, not just the municipality. We have a scheme to reimburse swimming lessons for children from low-income families. More attention might be needed here. Furthermore, this is also the responsibility of the parents.

122681.explanation: As far as we're concerned, no coffeeshop. Also because of the type of customers they attract.

122683.explanation: A single tall residential building near the new PC Hooftstraat or Trelleborg site is acceptable. That is the city center and fits with the apartment complexes around Vondelpark. Besides that, no.

122684.explanation: But then only under strict conditions. This can provide some relief in the housing market and opportunities for young people.

122689.explanation: We currently have about 38 percent social rental housing, more than neighboring municipalities, and we want to aim for the national average of 30 percent.

122632.explanation: PvdA Ridderkerk wants to investigate whether a citizen's assembly can be used for important municipal issues.
As residents, we govern our own municipality. When an issue plays a major role, a referendum can be a fair and effective way to make a decision. That way, we involve people in local democracy.

122633.explanation: If you give people the trust to build something together in their own neighborhood, beautiful things arise. The municipality should not hinder this but rather encourage it.

122635.explanation: This also applies to whether or not stores are open on Sundays.

122636.explanation: This is the ideal picture: residents producing their own green and affordable electricity together with a wind turbine or solar panels. They use the electricity in the same place, so the grid is not burdened. Energy cooperatives are an asset to Ridderkerk, and we should encourage them.

122637.explanation: We need more housing and green spaces. With Donkersloot, Cornelisland, Nieuw Reijerwaard, and among others Panattoni by the river, we already have more than enough box-like buildings. They spoil the landscape, and much of the work is done by poorly paid migrant laborers.

122638.explanation: In Ridderkerk, there has long been an asylum boat without incidents, and with this we help people fleeing war and violence. Ridderkerk is for everyone; together we seek solutions. Solidarity, but strict where needed. People who do not abide by our rules of conduct do not belong here.

122640.explanation: Burning must be done according to the advice on the stove guide. If there's no wind, we do not burn.

122643.explanation: Garbage, old mattresses, and other litter on the street are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. The easiest way to prevent this is by collecting bulky waste for free. This avoids hassle and gives us cleaner streets.

122644.explanation: Ridderkerk has relatively few large events; events and livability can go hand in hand.

122646.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy and satisfaction. And it keeps you healthy. But for people with welfare benefits, sports are often too expensive. We believe the municipality should ensure this group can participate in a sports club for free.

122649.explanation: Children are under huge pressure, from addictive social media to performance pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. We want music lessons not only to be accessible to children with wealthy parents. This way, we offer every child equal opportunities.

122652.explanation: Recently, Ridderkerk has actually increased the number of enforcement officers (BOAs). Ridderkerk currently has a higher number of BOAs per capita compared to other municipalities. These officers must now be deployed in a targeted manner.

122653.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras are not the right solution in our opinion. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be violated lightly. Therefore, we prefer more street enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

122660.explanation: Vacant offices should definitely be converted into housing. The municipality must do everything possible to make this happen, but project developers do not need subsidies from the municipality for this, only support and assistance.

122661.explanation: The housing shortage is high. Especially with temporary (flex) housing, you temporarily resolve the greatest need.

122662.explanation: Many people are not on social assistance because they don't want to work. They need a stable base, affordable housing, trust from the municipality, and guidance towards a job.

122663.explanation: If the waiving of municipal taxes stops, people with the lowest incomes in Ridderkerk will face serious problems. They simply cannot afford that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water, which is why we support tax waivers.

122666.explanation: When you designate more parking spaces for electric cars, you stimulate electric driving. Ridderkerk currently has too few charging points and not enough reserved parking spots per charger for electric cars.

122667.explanation: The figures clearly show: a lower maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with reduced mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

122669.explanation: Clean air is not a luxury but a basic right. Polluting vehicles cause health problems, especially among children and the elderly. With an environmental zone, we improve air quality in the city center and make Ridderkerk healthier and even more pleasant to live in.

122670.explanation: Good care aligns with who you are. If a caregiver takes into account someone's religion, culture, or beliefs, people feel safer and better helped. This freedom of choice increases the quality and accessibility of care in Ridderkerk.

122671.explanation: Quality comes before price. The waiting lists and problems in youth assistance and youth care are enormous. We must especially ensure help that matches the concerns of our youth.

122673.explanation: GroenLinks does not support raising taxes. Property tax (ozb) is not an end in itself for us but a means to keep important facilities affordable. Therefore, we carefully consider what is needed, with attention to a fair distribution.

122676.explanation: Every shopkeeper should be able to decide for themselves which days they open. It should also be possible to open on Sundays in Ridderkerk.

122677.explanation: The housing shortage affects many residents. Status holders have the right to safe housing, but we must also be mindful of other home seekers. Therefore, we choose extra housing construction and faster turnover, so no one is played off against each other.

122680.explanation: The Netherlands is a water country and every child should learn to swim. Including children whose parents cannot afford swimming lessons. Unfortunately, this is still not fairly arranged. The best solution: school swimming for every child, with support from the municipality.

122681.explanation: A well-regulated and controlled coffeeshop is better than illegal trade. Besides, a coffeeshop only sells hash and weed, whereas with an illegal dealer you don't know what else they might offer.

122683.explanation: High-rise buildings are a solution to build more homes without sacrificing green space.

122684.explanation: We have a huge housing shortage, and many people in our municipality live more spaciously than necessary. That is why we want to make it easier to rent out individual rooms, provided they are well-maintained and rented for a fair price.

122689.explanation: Affordable housing is a basic right. Ridderkerk has a severe shortage of social rental housing. But we also focus on building for all target groups such as youth and seniors.

statements.title: Ridderkerk must invest more money in building social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Ridderkerk moet meer geld steken in de bouw van

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The residents of Ridderkerk should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Ridderkerk moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted in Ridderkerk.

titleParts.text: In Ridderkerk moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety should be deployed in Ridderkerk.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
There should be more camera surveillance in Ridderkerk.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality must invest in temporary housing for students, for example container housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in tijdelijke huisvesting voor studenten, bijvoorbeeld

titleParts.glossary: Moveable and temporary homes, usually made from a shipping container.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Ridderkerk should introduce an environmental zone to keep polluting vehicles out of the center.

titleParts.glossary: An area where very polluting cars are not allowed to drive.

titleParts.text: invoeren om vervuilende voertuigen uit het centrum te weren.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: More high-rise apartment buildings may be allowed in Ridderkerk.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality must not allow Sunday shopping.
